content.subtitle: All modal verbs
items: stay the same for every subject — there is no -s in the third person: She **can** drive. / He **should** call.
are always followed by a bare infinitive (without *to*): You **must wear** a helmet.
have no infinitive form of their own.

content.subtitle: Be careful
notes: To form a question, invert the modal and the subject: **Should** we leave now?
Modals can also be used in passive structures: The form **must be signed** by a parent.

rows.use: Ability in the present or in general
rows.example: My little sister **can** already ride a bike.
When he was young, he **could** run really fast.

content.subtitle: Be careful
notes: Use **be able to** when you need a tense that *can* does not have: It's great to **be able to** work from home.
Future: After more practice, you'll **be able to** speak Spanish fluently.
Present perfect: Have you **been able to** reach her on the phone?

rows.example: **May** I open the window? / **Could** I leave a few minutes early?
You **can** use the printer whenever you like.
You really **should** / **ought to** get more sleep if you're tired all the time.

content.subtitle: Be careful
notes: **May** sounds more formal and polite than **could**, and **could** is more polite than **can**.

sections.title: A: Complete with can, could or be able to
sections.instructions: Write the correct form in each gap.

items.sentence: I love music, but I ...... play the piano yet.
When my grandmother was five, she ...... already read.
After the operation, he'll ...... walk again soon.
We haven't ...... solve this maths problem yet.
...... you swim when you were six?
If you practise every day, you'll ...... speak more fluently.
Sorry, I ...... come to the party tonight.
She wasn't ...... finish the test on time.

sections.title: B: Permission (choose the correct modal)
sections.instructions: Circle the correct word or phrase.

items.sentence: **Can / May** I leave the classroom for a minute, please?
You **can / could** borrow my dictionary if you want.
**Could / Can** you open the window, please?
Students **may / could** use calculators in this part of the exam.
**May / Can** I ask you a personal question?
You **couldn't / can't** park here. It's private.
Excuse me, **could / may** you tell me where platform 3 is?
No, you **may not / could not** use your phone during the lesson.

sections.title: C: Advice (should / ought to)
sections.instructions: Complete each sentence with should, shouldn't, ought to or ought not to.

items.sentence: You look tired. You ...... go to bed earlier.
We ...... spend so much money on things we don't need.
If he wants to pass, he ...... study more regularly.
You ...... eat so many sweets if you want healthier teeth.
What ...... I do about this headache?
You ...... talk to your teacher if you're worried.
People ...... be rude to shop assistants.
We ...... leave now or we'll miss the bus.

sections.title: D: Rewrite using a modal
sections.instructions: Rewrite each sentence using the modal in brackets.

items.sentence:
Is it possible for you to help me this afternoon? (can)
...... you help me this afternoon?

It is not a good idea to stay up so late. (shouldn't)
You ...... stay up so late.

I had the ability to run very fast when I was younger. (could)
I ...... run very fast when I was younger.

Is it all right if I sit here? (may)
...... I sit here?

It is not necessary to shout. (shouldn't)
You ...... shout.

She has managed to finish the project. (been able to)
She has ...... finish the project.

sections.title: E: Key word transformation
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence so it means the same as the first.

items.sentence:
It's a bad idea to drink lots of coffee at night. **SHOULDN'T**
You ...... lots of coffee at night.

Is it OK if I use your laptop? **MAY**
...... your laptop?

When I was eight, I had the ability to ski. **COULD**
When I was eight, I ...... .

Soon she will manage to drive on her own. **ABLE**
Soon she will ...... drive on her own.

Do you think I should call him? **OUGHT**
Do you think I ...... him?

Please don't use your phone in class. **MUSTN'T**
You ...... your phone in class.
'I'm very worried about the exam.' 'You ...... so much. You'll be fine.'
When I was little, I ...... stay up late at weekends.
'...... I borrow your pen for a second?' 'Of course.'
You ...... bring your passport if you're flying abroad.
By next month, he ...... speak French much better.
Students ...... run in the corridors at school.

sections.title: G: Error correction
sections.instructions: Each sentence has one mistake with a modal. Write the correct sentence.

items.sentence: She cans speak three languages.
You should to see a doctor.
May I to use your phone for a moment?
items.answer: May I use your phone for a moment?

items.sentence: I didn't could understand the instructions.
He will can finish it tomorrow.
items.answer: He will be able to finish it tomorrow.

items.sentence: We ought not go there so late.
items.answer: We ought not to go there so late.

sections.title: H: Mixed practice
sections.instructions: Complete each gap with one suitable modal expression.

items.sentence: Excuse me, ...... I ask you something?
You ...... wear a helmet when you ride a bike.
I ...... read until I was seven years old.
Don't worry. I'm sure you'll ...... solve the problem.
...... you possibly repeat that, please?
We ...... arrive early if we want good seats.
Passengers ...... smoke on the plane.
My little brother ...... use a computer very well now.
